ddls:
CREATE TABLE "temperature_control_points" (
  "tc_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each temperature control reading */,
  "test_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the test associated with these readings */,
  "west_upper_tc" REAL /* Temperature at the upper west TC in degrees Celsius */,
  "west_middle_tc" REAL /* Temperature at the middle west TC in degrees Celsius */,
  "west_bottom_tc" REAL /* Temperature at the bottom west TC in degrees Celsius */,
  "west_tc_average" REAL /* Average temperature at the west TC points in degrees Celsius */,
  "east_upper_tc" REAL /* Temperature at the upper east TC in degrees Celsius */,
  "east_middle_tc" REAL /* Temperature at the middle east TC in degrees Celsius */,
  "east_bottom_tc" REAL /* Temperature at the bottom east TC in degrees Celsius */,
  "east_tc_average" REAL /* Average temperature at the east TC points in degrees Celsius */,
  "overall_average_tc" REAL /* Average temperature of all TC points in degrees Celsius */,
  "exit_duct_tc" REAL /* Temperature in the exit duct in degrees Celsius */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("tc_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., technician, engineer, admin) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department or team the user belongs to */,
  "job_title" TEXT /* Job title of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "compliance_records" (
  "compliance_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each compliance record */,
  "test_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the test associated with the compliance check */,
  "compliance_status" TEXT /* Status of compliance (e.g., compliant, non-compliant) */,
  "check_date" TEXT /* Date the compliance check was conducted */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Additional notes regarding the compliance check */,
  "compliance_standard" TEXT /* Compliance standard or regulation checked against */,
  "check_method" TEXT /* Method used for the compliance check */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("compliance_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_compliance_records_compliance_standard FOREIGN KEY ("compliance_standard") REFERENCES compliance_standards ("standard_name")
)

CREATE TABLE "test_protocols" (
  "protocol_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each testing protocol */,
  "protocol_name" TEXT /* Name of the testing protocol */,
  "version" TEXT /* Version of the protocol */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the testing methodology */,
  "creation_date" TEXT /* Date the protocol was created */,
  "update_history" TEXT /* History of updates to the protocol */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("protocol_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "equipment" (
  "equipment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each piece of equipment */,
  "equipment_name" TEXT /* Name of the equipment */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the equipment's function */,
  "calibration_date" TEXT /* Last calibration date of the equipment */,
  "manufacturer" TEXT /* Manufacturer of the equipment */,
  "model_number" TEXT /* Model number of the equipment */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("equipment_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "fuel_types" (
  "fuel_type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each fuel type */,
  "fuel_type_name" TEXT /* Name of the fuel type */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the fuel type */,
  "properties" TEXT /* Properties of the fuel type (e.g., energy density, viscosity) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("fuel_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "compliance_standards" (
  "standard_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each compliance standard */,
  "standard_name" TEXT /* Name of the compliance standard */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the compliance standard */,
  "regulation" TEXT /* Regulation or authority that governs the standard */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("standard_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "tests" (
  "test_id" INTEGER PRIMARY KEY /* Unique identifier for each test */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user who conducted the test */,
  "equipment_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the equipment used in the test */,
  "fuel_type_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the fuel type used in the test */,
  "protocol_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the protocol used for the test */,
  FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"),
  FOREIGN KEY ("equipment_id") REFERENCES equipment ("equipment_id"),
  FOREIGN KEY ("fuel_type_id") REFERENCES fuel_types ("fuel_type_id"),
  FOREIGN KEY ("protocol_id") REFERENCES test_protocols ("protocol_id")
)
